pages.title: Still Got 5 On It
pages.tagline: Five was enough back then. Now it's just dust.

pages.body: Step into a gritty East Coast boom bap world at 92 BPM, where dusty drums with heavy swing crack against thick vinyl hiss and foghorn sub-bass shadows. A sinister minor-key piano sets the noir mood while bright xylophone melodies doubled on mellow flute weave through raw confessional vocals from a raspy male lead. Gruff call-and-response ad-libs contrast sharply with a smooth, smoky R&B chorus delivering passionate vibrato in layered harmonies against muted trumpet and eerie synth motifs. This track is a melancholic reflection on how inflation devalues money while destroying community bonds, capturing nostalgia for an era where trust mattered more than price.
pages.styleSentence: A grimy East Coast boom bap track at 92 BPM featuring dusty drums with heavy swing and hard snare cracks over thick vinyl hiss, anchored by a foghorn sub-bass and sinister minor-key piano; the production lifts with a bright xylophone melody doubled on mellow flute that weaves through the hook to answer raw confessional vocals from a raspy male lead rapper supported by gruff call-and-response ad-libs, while a smooth smoky R&B chorus delivers passionate vibrato in layered harmonies against muted trumpet shadows and eerie glassy synth motifs.

pages.text:
[Intro — Projector Hiss, Distant Whistle]

[Low Male Voice]
Yeah...

Still got five on it.

[Second Voice]
Five?

[Low Male Voice]
Man, five used to mean somethin'.

[Beat Drops]

[Hook — Smoky Male R&B, Simple]

Still got five on it
Still got five on it
But five don't buy what five did

Still got five on it
Still got five on it
Man, tell me where the good days went

[Verse 1 — Rooklyn]

Five in the hand, that was enough back then
Little bag came through, everybody got in
One had the papers, one had the light
One little five kept the room good all night

Windows cracked, beat played low
Good herb hit before the first damn smoke
Sticky little fingers when you broke that green
You knew what it was, you knew what I mean

[Second Voice]
No label?

[Rooklyn]
Nah.

[Second Voice]
No fancy name?

[Rooklyn]
Nah.

Just good clean smoke and a pocket of change
Now five hit the table, everybody look strange

I said, “Lemme get five.”

He said—

[Beat Cuts]

“Five of what?”

[Hook — Bigger]

Still got five on it
Still got five on it
But five don't buy what five did

Still got five on it
Still got five on it
Man, tell me where the good days went

[Chorus — Layered, Sing-Along]

Five used to stretch
Five used to last
Five had the whole damn room on blast

Five used to stretch
Five used to live
Now five don't buy what five used to give

Still got five on it
Still got five on it
But five don't buy what five did

[Verse 2 — Rooklyn]

Now the price went up and the trust went down
Too much mystery smoke going round this town
What they spray on that?
What they mix in this?

[Second Voice]
Why it smell like candy and chemical mist?

[Rooklyn]
Nah, keep that bag
I remember them days
Biggest damn problem was finding a place
Somebody got hungry, somebody got loud
Somebody told stories and forgot half out

Now every little strain got a fourteen-word name
“Supernova Nuclear Purple Cocaine”

[Second Voice]
What?

[Rooklyn]
Exactly.

I don't need a barcode
I don't need a brand
I need five dollars' worth
Like five used to understand

[Hook — R&B]

Still got five on it
Still got five on it
But five don't buy what five did

Still got five on it
Still got five on it
Man, tell me where the good days went

[Bridge — Beat Strips Down]

[Male R&B Voice]
Good old days...
Pass it around

Good clean smoke...
Music turned down

Five in the middle...
Everybody chipped in

Now I put five down...

[Pause]

[Rooklyn]
...and they tell me, “Try again.”

[Beat Slams Back]

[Final Chorus — Huge but Grim]

Five used to stretch
Five used to last
Five had the whole damn room on blast

Five used to stretch
Five used to live
Now five don't buy what five used to give

Still got five on it
Still got five on it
But five don't buy what five did

Still got five on it
Still got five on it
Man, tell me where the good days went

[Final Hook — Repeating]

Still got five on it
(Still got five)

Still got five on it
(Still got five)

But five don't buy what five did

Still got five on it
(Still got five)

Still got five on it

[Rooklyn — Spoken]
...Might as well buy chips.

[Second Voice]
Small bag.

[Rooklyn]
Man, fuck 2026.

[Projector Click]

[End]

pages.vocalStyle: raspy male lead with raw confessional tone alternating with gruff call-and-response ad-libs, contrasted by a smooth smoky R&B chorus featuring passionate vibrato and layered harmonies

members.name: LEAD VOX
members.bio: Raspy voice confesses the cost of inflation with raw honesty.

members.name: DRUMS
members.bio: Keeps the floor shaking. Never misses a downbeat or crack.

members.name: TEXTURES
members.bio: Weaves flute, trumpet shadows, and synth motifs into the fog.

levels.name: WEARY WISHES
levels.emotion: Confused longing and weary resignation as the narrator questions where value has gone.

levels.name: MEMORY MIRROR
levels.emotion: Raw storytelling and bittersweet memory, contrasting past unity with current isolation.

levels.name: SPIRITLESS FIVE
levels.emotion: Aggressive lamentation, repeating the core truth that money no longer holds its spiritual or communal weight.

levels.name: HONEST PLEA
levels.emotion: Urgent plea for return to shared values and simple, honest connection.

levels.name: CYNICAL ACCEPTANCE
levels.emotion: Defeated acceptance and cynical dismissal of repeated attempts to fix what is broken.

levels.name: ERODING VALUE
levels.emotion: Resigned observation of value eroding over time, accepting that 'five' is now dead.
